description: in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, India
description_wiki: Ewing Christian College (ECC), formerly Allahabad Christian College, is an autonomous constituent college of University of Allahabad, located in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, India.

description: great Turkic descent empire ruling India (1526–1857)
description_wiki: The Mughal Empire was an early modern empire in South Asia.

description: civil service of India during British rule from 1858 to 1947
description_wiki: The Indian Civil Service (abbreviated as ICS, officially known as the Imperial Civil Service) was the higher civil service of the British Raj in the period between 1858 and 1947.

description: country in South Asia
description_wiki: India, officially the Republic of India, is a country in South Asia.

description: bacterial infectious disorder contracted by consumption of food or drink contaminated with Salmonella typhi. This disorder is common in developing countries and can be treated with antibiotics.
description_wiki: Typhoid fever, also known as typhoid, is an orally contracted disease caused by Salmonella enterica serotype Typhi bacteria, also called Salmonella Typhi.

description: 1918–1920 global influenza pandemic caused by the H1N1 influenza A virus
country in East Asia
description_wiki: China, officially the People's Republic of China (PRC), is a country in East Asia.

description: widespread scarcity of food followed by regional malnutrition, starvation, epidemic, and increased mortality
description_wiki: A famine is a widespread scarcity of food caused by several possible factors, including, but not limited to: war, natural disasters, crop failure, widespread poverty, an economic catastrophe or government policies.

description: period in the history of the Republic of China
description_wiki: The Warlord Era was the period in the history of the Republic of China between 1916 and 1928, when control of the country was divided between rival military cliques of the Beiyang Army and other regional factions.

description: public university in Beijing, China
description_wiki: Tsinghua University (THU) is a public university in Haidian, Beijing, China.

description: railway line between Moscow, the Russian Far East and the Sea of Japan
description_wiki: The Trans-Siberian Railway, historically known as the Great Siberian Route and often shortened to Transsib, is a large railway system that connects European Russia to the Russian Far East.

description: Prefecture-level and sub-provincial city in Liaoning, China
description_wiki: Shenyang, formerly known by its Manchu name Mukden, is a sub-provincial city in China and the provincial capital of Liaoning province.

description: capital and most populous city of Russia
description_wiki: Moscow is the capital and largest city of Russia, standing on the Moskva River in Central Russia.

description: economic policy of Soviet Russia proposed by Vladimir Lenin, who described it as a progression towards "state capitalism" within the workers' state of the USSR
description_wiki: The New Economic Policy (NEP) (Russian: новая экономическая политика (НЭП), romanized: novaya ekonomicheskaya politika) was an economic policy of the Soviet Union proposed by V. I. Lenin in 1921 as a temporary expedient.

description: Russian revolutionary and politician (1869-1939)
description_wiki: Nadezhda Konstantinovna Krupskaya (Russian: Надежда Константиновна Крупская, IPA: [nɐˈdʲeʐdə kənstɐnʲˈtʲinəvnə ˈkrupskəjə]; 26 February [O.S. 14 February] 1869 – 27 February 1939) was a Russian revolutionary, politician and political theorist.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: group of macroeconomic theories
description_wiki: Keynesian economics ( KAYN-zee-ən; sometimes Keynesianism, named after British economist John Maynard Keynes) are the various macroeconomic theories and models of how aggregate demand (total spending in the economy) strongly influences economic output and inflation.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)
description_wiki: Harry S. Truman  (May 8, 1884 – December 26, 1972) was the 33rd president of the United States, serving from 1945 to 1953.

aliases: Resettlement Administration of the United States of America
Resettlement Administration Division of Suburban Settlement
description: New Deal agency relocated urban and rural families to United States federal government planned communities
description_wiki: The Resettlement Administration (RA) was a New Deal U.S. federal agency created May 1, 1935.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Social Security Board may refer to:

description: U.S. federal executive department overseeing commerce
description_wiki: The United States Department of Commerce (DOC) is an executive department of the U.S. federal government.

description: United States of America government agency
description_wiki: The War Production Board (WPB) was an agency of the United States government that supervised war production during World War II. President Franklin D. Roosevelt established it in January 1942, with Executive Order 9024.

description: American initiative for foreign aid to Western Europe following World War II
description_wiki: The Marshall Plan (officially the European Recovery Program, ERP) was an American initiative enacted in 1948 to provide foreign aid to Western Europe.

description: intergovernmental military alliance between 32 member states
public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: independent agency and volunteer program run by the United States Government
description_wiki: The Peace Corps is an independent agency and program of the United States government that trains and deploys volunteers to communities in partner countries around the world.

description: 1947–1991 tension between the Soviet Union and the United States and their respective allies
description_wiki: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc.
